channel.title: Core Design :: CS Two Year Refactor
channel.description: What the curriculum IS: the eight-block spine, the thirteen threads (nine spirals, three lenses, one bounded practice), and the program overview.

item.title: Program Overview
item.description: Working draft for faculty review. This is the front door to a set of 22 companion files: 8 block files, 9 thread files, 3 lens files, and 1 bounded-practice file. Course codes throughout are placeholders. What this is A competency-based, systems-thinking foundation for a B.S. in Computer Science, shared by students who will go on to specialize (Cybersecurity, Data Science, AI Systems, Software Architecture). It is built as a spiral curriculum: ideas recur across the two years, each return deepening or generalizing the last, rather than being taught once and left behind.

item.title: Blocks
item.description: The eight 8-week blocks: Read → Modify → Model → Structure → Store → Build Responsibly → Judge → Operate.

item.title: Threads
item.description: Nine spiraling threads and one bounded practice (AI-Assisted Development).

item.title: Lenses
item.description: Three lenses applied across blocks: Trustworthy Computing, Optimization Reasoning, Professional Practices.
